---
title: Node Classification
subtitle: classifying network nodes by type and connectivity
description: A short description
author:
 - name: Pukar Bhandari
   email: pukar.bhandari@wfrc.utah.gov
   affiliation:
     - name: Wasatch Front Regional Council
       url: "https://wfrc.utah.gov/"
date: "2026-03-13"
---

## Setup Environment

### Import Libraries

```{python}
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd()))
from _src.node_utils import classify_nodes, count_node_links
```

### Filepath Configuration

```{python}
config_filepath = {
    # Input files
    "in_centerlines": Path("_data/raw/UtahRoads.gpkg"),
    "in_network_links": Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Link.gpkg"),
    "in_network_nodes": Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Node.gpkg"),
    # Output files
    "out_node_classified": Path("_data/processed/nodes_classified.gpkg"),
}
```

## Load Data

### Roadway Centerlines

```{python}
gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file(config_filepath["in_centerlines"])

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_centerlines.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Links

```{python}
gdf_links = gpd.read_file(config_filepath["in_network_links"])

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_links):,} features | CRS: {gdf_links.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_links.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Nodes

```{python}
gdf_nodes = gpd.read_file(config_filepath["in_network_nodes"])

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_nodes):,} features | CRS: {gdf_nodes.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_nodes.head(1000).explore()
```

## Node Classification

### Classification Config

Each entry defines one output column. `criteria` is a list of conditions
combined with `"combine": "AND"` or `"OR"` (defaults to `"OR"` if omitted).

Supported criteria types:

- `{"type": "ft", "values": [...]}` — node appears in A or B of a link whose
  FT column matches one of the given values
- `{"type": "n_range", "ranges": [(lo, hi), ...]}` — node N falls within any
  of the given ranges, inclusive on both ends

```{python}
CLASSIFICATION = {
    "Freeway": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": list(range(20, 28)) + list(range(30, 40))}]},
    "Expressway": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": [12, 13, 14, 15]}]},
    "Arterial": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": [2, 3]}]},
    "Collector": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": [4, 5]}]},
    "Local": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": [6, 7]}]},
    "Ramp": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": [28, 29, 41, 42]}]},
    "CentroidConnector": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": [1]}]},
    # Two independent FixedTransit columns to compare methods.
    # "FixedTransit_byFT": {"criteria": [{"type": "ft", "values": [70, 80]}]},
    # "FixedTransit_byN": {
    #     "criteria": [{"type": "n_range", "ranges": [(10_000, 19_999), (50_000, 59_999)]}]
    # },
    # Combine into one column with AND
    "FixedTransit": {
        "criteria": [
            {"type": "ft", "values": [70, 80]},
            {"type": "n_range", "ranges": [(10_000, 19_999), (50_000, 59_999)]},
        ],
        "combine": "AND",
    },
}
```

### Classify Nodes

```{python}
gdf_nodes_classified = classify_nodes(
    gdf_nodes,
    gdf_links,
    classification=CLASSIFICATION,
    ft_year=2023,
)

classification_cols = ["N"] + list(CLASSIFICATION.keys())
gdf_nodes_classified[classification_cols].head(20)
```

### Review FixedTransit Methods

Compare the two independent FixedTransit columns to decide which to keep.

```{python}
ft_comparison = gdf_nodes_classified[
    ["N", "FixedTransit_byFT", "FixedTransit_byN"]
].copy()
ft_comparison["agree"] = (
    ft_comparison["FixedTransit_byFT"] == ft_comparison["FixedTransit_byN"]
)

# Inspect any disagreeing rows
ft_comparison[~ft_comparison["agree"]]
```

### Summary

```{python}
gdf_nodes_classified[list(CLASSIFICATION.keys())].sum().rename("NodeCount").to_frame()
```

## Link Count

### Count Links per Node

```{python}
gdf_nodes_classified = count_node_links(gdf_nodes_classified, gdf_links)

gdf_nodes_classified[["N", "LinkCount"]].head(20)
```

### Distribution of Link Counts

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

link_count_freq = gdf_nodes_classified["LinkCount"].value_counts().sort_index()
max_val = link_count_freq.max()

# -- Colors
BASE_COLOR = "#B4B2A9"  # c-gray 200  — default
ACCENT_COLOR = "#1D9E75"  # c-teal 400  — typical intersection nodes (4, 6, 8)
PSEUDO_COLOR = "#EF9F27"  # c-amber 400 — pseudonodes (2 links)
LABEL_COLOR = "#444441"  # c-gray 800
SUBTITLE_COLOR = "#888780"  # c-gray 400
GRID_COLOR = "#D3D1C7"  # c-gray 100

ACCENT_COUNTS = {4, 6, 8}
PSEUDO_COUNTS = {2}


def bar_color(link_count):
    if link_count in PSEUDO_COUNTS:
        return PSEUDO_COLOR
    if link_count in ACCENT_COUNTS:
        return ACCENT_COLOR
    return BASE_COLOR


bar_colors = [bar_color(i) for i in link_count_freq.index]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5.5))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")

ax.yaxis.grid(True, color=GRID_COLOR, linewidth=0.6, zorder=0)
ax.set_axisbelow(True)

bars = ax.bar(link_count_freq.index, link_count_freq.values, color=bar_colors, width=0.6, zorder=2)

for bar in bars:
    h = bar.get_height()
    ax.text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
        h + max_val * 0.012,
        f"{int(h):,}",
        ha="center",
        va="bottom",
        fontsize=9.5,
        color=LABEL_COLOR,
        fontweight="normal",
    )

for spine in ["top", "right", "left"]:
    ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(GRID_COLOR)

ax.set_xticks(link_count_freq.index)
ax.set_xticklabels(link_count_freq.index, fontsize=11, color=LABEL_COLOR)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}"))
ax.tick_params(axis="y", labelsize=9.5, colors=SUBTITLE_COLOR, length=0)
ax.tick_params(axis="x", length=0)
ax.set_ylim(0, max_val * 1.18)

ax.set_xlabel("Number of links connected to node", fontsize=10.5, color=SUBTITLE_COLOR, labelpad=10)
ax.set_ylabel("")

# -- Inline-colored legend in subtitle style
legend_elements = [
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=ACCENT_COLOR),
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=PSEUDO_COLOR),
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=BASE_COLOR),
]
ax.legend(
    legend_elements,
    ["Typical intersection (4, 6, 8)", "Pseudonode (2)", "Other"],
    loc="upper right",
    fontsize=9,
    frameon=False,
    labelcolor=SUBTITLE_COLOR,
    handlelength=1,
    handleheight=0.8,
)

fig.text(
    0.13,
    1.01,
    "Most nodes connect to 4, 6, or 8 links",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold",
    color=LABEL_COLOR,
    transform=ax.transAxes,
)
fig.text(
    0.13,
    0.94,
    f"Distribution of {len(gdf_nodes_classified):,} network nodes by link count",
    fontsize=10.5,
    color=SUBTITLE_COLOR,
    transform=ax.transAxes,
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Checkpoint

```{python}
out_path = config_filepath["out_node_classified"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes_classified.to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Checkpoint saved to: {out_path}")
```
